risks: 1. Grep for 'category' will hit false positives — furnitureCatalog.ts, game pixel-types, ActionPanel, LogPanel, StatusPanel all have unrelated 'category' fields for game furniture/items. Builder must be careful to only remove directive/project category references, not game-domain ones.

2. MCP backlog tool uses category as a required parameter in addBacklogItem and optional filter in listBacklog. Removing it changes the tool's API contract — any existing MCP clients or prompts referencing the category parameter will break silently.

3. Server compilation — category is woven through work-item-types.ts (4 Zod schemas), aggregator, watchers, and types.ts. Missing one reference will cause TypeScript compile errors that block the server.
over_engineering_flags: This is already scoped tightly — pure removal, no replacement concept. No over-engineering risk. The directive is appropriately minimal.
challenges.recommendation: Proceed as-is. This is a clean removal with well-defined scope. Single project is sufficient since all changes are deletions and the work is straightforward.

projects.title: Remove category field from framework
projects.scope_summary: Strip the category field from all directive.json files, server types/watchers/aggregator, MCP tools (status grouping, backlog filtering/adding), frontend store types, pipeline docs/schemas/templates, validate-project-json.sh hook, and CLAUDE.md. All changes are field removals and reference deletions. The MCP backlog add tool drops category as a required param; backlog list drops category filter. Server must compile cleanly after removal. Game furniture category fields are explicitly untouched.
